player_notes.mitchell robinson: Upgraded from questionable to available for Game 1 after hand surgery; practiced Tuesday and cleared to play. Monitor minutes as he returns from injury — potential restriction risk.

game_notes.NYK vs SAS: Mitchell Robinson (broken finger) now available for Game 1 after being listed questionable; his return restores NYK's backup center depth and could limit Karl-Anthony Towns' foul-trouble exposure.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The season context document covers R1 and does not include R2, ECF, WCF, or Finals results. The playoff bracket shows (3) NYK vs (6) ATL and (2) SAS vs (7) POR as R1 matchups with no Finals context.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple news items confirm the 2026 NBA Finals are underway between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs, with Game 1 scheduled for Wednesday June 4 in San Antonio. This means both NYK and SAS advanced through R1, R2, and their respective conference finals — none of which are documented in the season context.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Finals context: NYK and SAS have both advanced to the 2026 NBA Finals. Game 1 is June 4 in San Antonio (SAS is the home team and series favorite per odds). Document R2, ECF, WCF series results and any player injuries/role changes that occurred across those rounds. The entire playoff diary from R2 onward is missing.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Mitchell Robinson is not mentioned in the season context document as an active player or injury concern. The document notes 'ELIMINATED' teams and injury landscape but has no Robinson entry.

suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Mitchell Robinson to the injury landscape / player notes: he had hand/finger surgery at some point during the playoffs, was questionable for Finals G1, but has been cleared and is available. His availability as NYK's backup center is relevant for prop context (Jericho Sims or other center depth minutes may shift).

suggested_context_updates.conflict: News items describe Harper as a 'rookie sensation' with 'Best of Dylan Harper in the NBA Playoffs' coverage and an article about what an NBA championship would mean to the Philippines for Harper and Jordan Clarkson — framing him as a star-level contributor. Additionally, Jordan Clarkson is referenced as a NYK player ('Best of Jordan Clarkson in the NBA Playoffs' for the Knicks), which is not reflected in the season context roster at all.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: 1) Update Harper's role note — his R2+ production and role expansion through the conference finals are not documented and may be significantly elevated from his R1 profile. 2) Investigate and add Jordan Clarkson to the NYK roster — he appears to be an active NYK contributor in the Finals run and is entirely absent from the season context document.

suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Jordan Clarkson to the NYK roster in the season context. Determine when he joined NYK (likely a trade or signing after the context doc's last roster update) and document his role. His playoff contributions through the Finals run need to be captured.

suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Brunson's injury status to fully healthy. Remove the left ankle concern flag. Document his health and production through R2 and ECF in the playoff diary.
